items.title: 'Devoted father and gentleman': Tributes flow for shark attack victim

children.content: The wife of a 38-year-old Perth man who died in a shark attack off the coast of Rottnest Island in Western Australia on Saturday describes him as a devoted father and avid spearfisherman.

gallery.altText: A man holding a little girl and a woman holding a baby with the beach in the background
A man on a boat holding a large fish.

thumbnail.altText: A man holding a little girl and a woman holding a baby with the beach in the background
A man on a boat holding a large fish.

items.title: Treasurer stands by CGT changes affecting shares and crypto

children.content: Jim Chalmers says it is better for young people to invest based on economic rather than tax outcomes, and argued they can still access negative gearing by buying up new homes.

image.altText: Jim Chalmers sits in a row of people in parliament. He leans forward and looks towards the camera.

items.title: Labor claims victory in Queensland's Stafford by-election

children.content: Labor candidate Luke Richmond claims victory over the LNP's Fiona Hammond in a tight race in the north Brisbane electorate of Stafford.

image.altText: A man in a red polo looking to right of frame.

items.title: Qantas plane diverted after passenger allegedly bites flight attendant

children.content: Fellow passengers are believed to have assisted after a flight attendant was allegedly bitten on plane travelling between Melbourne and the US, with the plane diverted to Tahiti.

image.altText: Two men face each other in a plane

items.title: Protesters march at Nakba Day rallies around Australia

children.content: Crowds gather at rallies around the country to commemorate the anniversary of the Nakba, a day that recognises the mass displacement of Palestinians in 1948.

image.altText: A agthering of people on a city street with some holding Palestinian flags.

items.title: From Euro-pop to Euro-slop, the key moments from Eurovision as Delta makes Australia proud

children.content: Slept through Eurovision this year? Here's what you missed from Europe's favourite song contest, with boycotts, ballads and a Bulgarian belter called Bangaranga making headlines.

articles.title: Delta Goodrem takes fourth spot in Eurovision 2026 as Bulgaria wins

children.content: Delta Goodrem’s vocals secure a fourth-place finish for Australia in the annual contest, with Bulgaria taking top spot.

image.altText: Delta holding Australian flag and smiling

items.title: Giants hunting back-to-back wins in Eagles clash, Saints down Tigers

children.content: Round 10 of the AFL concludes as West Coast welcomes GWS to Perth Stadium, after St Kilda makes light work of Richmond at Docklands. Follow live.

items.title: NSW suffer major Origin blow, Warriors thrash Broncos in Brisbane

children.content: South Sydney and NSW star Latrell Mitchell is ruled out of State of Origin I after aggravating his back injury during Magic Round. Follow live.

image.altText: A rugby league player in red and green is on his haunches on the field.

items.title: You could hear a pin drop when Rob revealed why he fought a fire tax

children.content: Every time farmer and volunteer firefighter Rob Armstrong gets a call to battle a bushfire, something inside says not to do it. But he goes anyway. He knows what it’s like to be trapped in the flames and rescued by volunteers.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a navy blue long-sleeved shirt and brimmed hat with arms crossed. Behind imagery shows firefighters and bushfire

items.title: Ebola kills 87 people as cases continue to rise in Congo

children.content: At least 87 deaths have been reported in Congo's new Ebola disease outbreak, as health authorities warn the strain "has no vaccine, no specific treatment".

image.altText: A man takes the temperature of another man using a thermometer. He's wearing a mask.

items.title: How Pokémon cards are fuelling crime in Melbourne

children.content: Rare Pokémon cards can sell for hundreds or even thousands of dollars, with a record sale of one Pikachu card going for over $22 million earlier this year.

image.altText: Alex stands in front of a counter in a trading card shop store filled with stock

items.title: Sydney lawyer was stressed out by her mortgage so she sold up and bought a van

children.content: Phoebe Macdougall says she hated living with a mortgage, so she is trying van life instead.

items.title: AI psychosis is on the rise and experts are sounding the alarm

children.content: Rodrigues thought an AI chatbot could help him escape poverty and build wealth. Instead, he became trapped in a spiral of delusion and dependence.

image.altText: Robot hand and human hand touch fingers echoing Michaelangelo's painting Creation of Adam

items.title: Six shops ordered to shut under new illegal tobacco laws

children.content: More than 140,000 illegal cigarettes have been seized and six stores ordered to close down, days after Western Australia's new tobacco laws came into force.

image.altText: A shop front with 'store closed' banners stuck on the front windows

items.title: Love story: How a punk grandma and an Aussie rocker fell in love

children.content: Singer/guitarist Mick Medew and self-proclaimed punk grandma Ursula Collie were in their mid-50s when they met and bonded over a mutual love of live music. A decade on, they're musical and life partners.

gallery.altText: Mick Medew wears a black suit and white short and Ursula Collie wears a white dress on their wedding day. Both are smiling.

thumbnail.altText: Mick Medew wears a black suit and white short and Ursula Collie wears a white dress on their wedding day. Both are smiling.

items.title: ‘I can still feel him’: Gurrumul’s posthumous honour brings pride for family

children.content: Nearly a decade since his death, Gurrumul is set to be inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame, bringing pride and reflection to a remote part of Australia.

image.altText: 'The Gurrumul documentary gives everyone in sight that no-one really had before,' says friend and manager Michael Hohnen

items.title: Test your homophones and your Gen Alpha slang

children.content: Are you ready to test your spelling skills like your little lunch depends on it? Come on in.

items.title: 'No-one really questioned why' Olympic gymnast missed periods

children.content: Alexandra Kiroi-Bogatyreva trained up to 10 hours a day, six days a week, often without the proper nutrition. She wants others to know what that can do to the body.

gallery.altText: A gymnast does the splits while jumping and holding a ball.

thumbnail.altText: A gymnast does the splits while jumping and holding a ball.

items.title: Why some Australian homebuyers are turning to Islamic financing

children.content: When Wildan decided to buy a property he didn't even consider a conventional loan. Instead, he opted for a niche option that caters to a growing segment of Australia's population.

image.altText: Picture of Muslim family of four, wearing islamic attire and smiling to the camera.

items.title: What life was like before the NDIS and why mistakes can't be repeated

children.content: The states are once again on the hook for disability services due to the Commonwealth's drastic NDIS cuts. Can the mistakes of the past be avoided?

image.altText: A middle-aged man looking out to a gated, prison-like area

items.title: Hobart military barracks slated for housing development

children.content: Up to 1,000 new homes could be built on the site of a military barracks in Hobart, under a state-federal redevelopment agreement.

image.altText: A render of a proposed housing development at Dowsing Point, Hobart.

items.title: The budget hammers the final nail into the coffin of the Howard-era tax breaks

children.content: At the turn of the century, "aspiration" described Australians who wanted to put their kids into private school and university, and to build their wealth by getting an investment property or two. Today, it means something humbler; millions of younger Australians simply aspire to own a home one day, to have some shelter.

image.altText: John Howard and Peter Costello hold a media conference.

items.title: The darkness of a Tasmanian town's night sky could be protected

children.content: The northern Tasmanian town of Ross is working to protect its night sky, a years-long process that would see it formally recognised for its darkness and clarity.

gallery.altText: A church at night with a starry sky in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A church at night with a starry sky in the background.

items.title: From cask to car, could red wine be Australia's next fuel?

children.content: Australia's wine industry is investigating whether its current 263 million litre glut of wine would be better used as biofuel to power cars, trucks or even aircraft.

image.altText: A posed photo of someone supposedly pouring wine into the petrol inlet of a white car.

items.title: China quietly turns off supply of world's most used industrial chemical

children.content: Amid disruption from the Iran war, China has banned exports of sulphuric acid, a crucial component used to treat drinking water, refine petroleum and make batteries, clothing and phosphate fertilisers.

image.altText: beakers of coloured and clear liquid on a metal work bench both have glass measuring instruments in them one is being picked up

items.title: Pro-Palestine march comes face-to-face with anti-immigration protest in London

children.content: Tens of thousands of people have marched through central London in two separate protests: one against immigration and another in support of Palestinians.

image.altText: Protesters marching, one man is holding up a big gold key with the words 'we will return' on it.

items.title: Trump is painting DC's reflecting pool. Here's why people are angry

children.content: Donald Trump has chosen a new colour for Washington DC's Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. Here are three reasons it's proving to be controversial.

image.altText: The Lincoln Memorial is seen behind an empty reflecting pool, which is partially painted blue.

items.title: Bushwalker takes photo of rare bug in the wild

children.content: There were no known photographs taken in the wild of the
Myrmeleon houstoni
until a regional Victorian woman snapped a photo of one while on a bushwalk.

items.title: Top Hamas military leader killed in Israeli air strikes, official confirms

children.content: Izz al-Din al-Haddad is the most senior Hamas official to be killed by Israel since a US-backed ceasefire agreement meant to halt fighting was implemented in October.

image.altText: The shell of a car on fire with people crowding around.

items.title: WA's first pair of fainting goats settle into new home

children.content: The goats, which "faint" when stressed or excited, have travelled to a farm near Busselton, where they will be bred as pets.

items.title: First home buyers 'up and about' at auctions after negative gearing changes

children.content: The government says the changes are aimed at levelling the playing field for young buyers bidding against property investors, while the opposition is pledging to undo the reforms, citing their potential to raise rents.

image.altText: A row of houses on a suburban street in Melbourne, in what looks like a colder month.

items.title: Woman dead for weeks found in Hobart home

children.content: The body of a woman who has been dead for weeks has been found at a house in the Hobart suburb of New Town.

image.altText: Three police officers stand under a small shelter in a suburban street

items.title: Disabled Australians on love, lust and the right to intimacy

children.content: It's a stormy February night in Adelaide, and Jacqueline Tedmanson is at the hospital with her partner Jackson, waiting to meet their first baby.
